reasoning: Munkres defines "regular space" to include the T₁ axiom ("suppose one-point sets are closed... X is said to be regular if..."), whereas Mathlib's `RegularSpace` class (at `Mathlib/Topology/Separation/Regular.lean:79`) only asserts the separation of closed sets from points and does NOT include T₁. So to faithfully capture Munkres' notion of "regular," one needs `[RegularSpace X] [T1Space X]` — this is surfacing an implicit premise in Munkres' definition, not adding an extra restriction. Without T₁ the claim is actually false (consider the indiscrete topology on two points, which is `RegularSpace` but where closures of any nonempty opens are not disjoint). The `x ≠ y` hypothesis matches "pair of points" (which implicitly means distinct, else the conclusion is impossible). The conclusion `∃ U V open, x ∈ U, y ∈ V, closure U ∩ closure V = ∅` correctly captures "neighborhoods whose closures are disjoint." Thus the formalization is faithful.
